purpose: test whether the CF-I-O3 failure belongs to HUGSIM metadata-box geometry or persists when the rendered Gaussian asset envelope and origin are projected

projection_model.origin: quantile envelope center transformed by the exact planner body-to-world matrix
projection_model.qualification_boundary: asset-derived internal envelope, not a real vehicle box or external truth

controls.empty_actor_pair: render each selected state again with the dynamic actor map removed while camera pose and scene remain fixed

indicators.CF-I-O1: unchanged planner state to render transform control
indicators.CF-I-O2: unchanged camera membership control using the asset envelope projection
indicators.CF-I-O3: paired RGB support inside the projected opacity-qualified Gaussian envelope, retaining the 0.90 gate and shifted-projection negative

decision_logic.asset_envelope_o3_accepted: supports metadata-box mismatch as the source of the CF-I-OBS-002 localization rejection within this window
decision_logic.asset_envelope_o3_rejected: indicates a deeper asset-envelope to RGB projection mismatch or an unqualified envelope definition
decision_logic.metadata_box_result: remains rejected regardless of this result

stop_rule: after this bounded split, close first-round CF-I with both the metadata-box and asset-envelope decisions; do not tune the envelope or threshold again in this phase
independence_notice: The envelope is independently derived from the Gaussian asset rather than obj_boxes, but both the asset and RGB are HUGSIM-produced. This is an internal attribution test, not external sensor truth.
strongest_allowed_claim: The experiment may attribute the bounded localization failure to metadata-box geometry versus the rendered-asset envelope; it cannot establish real-camera or real-vehicle geometry equivalence.
forbidden_claims: the Gaussian envelope is a real vehicle ground-truth box
HUGSIM RGB is equivalent to a real camera
the AttackPlanner interaction is realistic traffic behavior
AD perception, planning, control, or safety is credible
HUGSIM is generally credible as an AD test domain
